pattern: To get the lexicographically smallest non-palindrome, greedily change the first non-'a' character outside the center to 'a'; if that is impossible (all 'a's), change the last character to 'b'.
hints: A single-character string can never be made non-palindromic with one replacement — that is the only impossible case.
To minimize lexicographic rank, scan left to right and try to replace the first character that is not 'a' with 'a'. Changing a character before the midpoint always breaks the palindrome property.
If every non-middle character is already 'a' (all-'a' string, or odd-length all-'a'), changing anything at the front keeps it a palindrome — instead, set the last character to 'b'.

edge_inputs.description: two identical characters — change first to 'a' would keep palindrome so change last to 'b'
first non-'a' is not at center
odd length all 'a' — only last-to-b works
odd-length where center is the only non-'a'; must skip it and change last to 'b'
non-'a' near the end but change of first non-'a' before midpoint is valid
length-2 palindrome of different letters — change first to 'a'
palindrome starting with 'a' but has non-'a' later before midpoint

stress.description: random even-length palindromes up to length 1000
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    half = max(1, size // 2)
    letters = [chr(ord('a') + rng.randrange(0, 26)) for _ in range(half)]
    s = ''.join(letters) + ''.join(reversed(letters))
    return [s]
